target_game.name: Flappy Dunk
target_game.core_loop: Tap to keep the avatar airborne, navigate through hoops to score points, and outscore the opponent in a head-to-head match.
target_game.audience_proxy: Casual gamers of all ages looking for quick, reflex-based competitive gameplay.
target_game.visual_style: 2D cartoon style with thick black outlines, flat shading, and vibrant, high-contrast colors.

palette.description: Warm beige backgrounds contrasted with highly saturated magenta banners and bright blue accents.

top_archetypes.label: Other · animation

centroid_hook.summary: Static character art of Goku Ultra Instinct with a prominent 'PLAY NOW!' call to action.
centroid_hook.visual_action: Goku is depicted charging a blue energy blast against a vibrant, rainbow-colored background with glowing orbs.
centroid_hook.text_overlay: Dragon Ball Z Dokkan Battle PLAY NOW!

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 620d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "Static character art of Goku Ultra Instinct with a prominent 'PLAY NOW!' call to"

centroid_hook.voiceover_transcript: Hey! Have you ever played a game that actually makes you feel relaxed?

top_archetypes.label: Fail · in-game

centroid_hook.summary: A player attempts a difficult level and immediately fails by crashing a bus.
centroid_hook.visual_action: A hand cursor moves a purple bus, then crashes a red bus into a green one.
centroid_hook.text_overlay: WHY THIS LEVEL IS SO HARD, CAN YOU WIN?

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 259d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "A player attempts a difficult level and immediately fails by crashing a bus."

game_fit_scores.rationale: This is a static IP-driven anime hook for Dragon Ball Z Dokkan Battle — it leans on licensed character recognition and a gacha/RPG audience, neither of which Flappy Dunk can authentically replicate. Visually the saturated rainbow + neon palette clashes with Flappy Dunk's warm beige/magenta cartoon look, and mechanically a static charging-energy-blast frame communicates nothing about tap-to-fly hoop physics or VS scoring. Skip this archetype: borrowing the format risks IP/legal friction and would mis-target anime-RPG users who churn fast on hypercasual arcade loops.

Hard pass. The Dragon Ball anime power-fantasy palette (gold/navy/indigo) and licensed-IP hero hook have zero overlap with Flappy Dunk's flat 2D cartoon basketball aesthetic, magenta/beige palette, or its tap-timing arcade loop — there's no way to bridge a Goku punch animation to hoop-tapping gameplay without misleading users and tanking IPM. Additionally, leveraging Goku/Dragon Ball IP is a trademark/copyright liability that ad networks and Apple will likely reject, and the shonen anime male-teen audience skews far narrower than Flappy Dunk's all-ages casual base.

Major friction: the bus-sorting puzzle hook telegraphs a slow, "relaxing" sort/puzzle game, which directly contradicts Flappy Dunk's energetic, reflex-based tap-to-fly competitive loop — viewers tapping in expecting a sorter will bounce on first session. Visually the cool blue/purple/orange member palette and stylus-hand POV clash with the warm-beige + magenta cartoon arcade DNA and the missing avatar/VS framing. Audience overlap is the only weak bridge (broad casual hypercasual users), so do NOT adopt this archetype as-is; at most, borrow only the "Hey! Have you ever played..." VO opener and reskin with hoop-chaining combo footage.

This archetype is a hard mismatch for Flappy Dunk: the muted earth-tone palette (navy/burnt orange/forest green) and architectural cross-section animation directly contradict the game's flat 2D cartoon style with vibrant magenta/beige/blue and a tappable avatar. There is also no plausible bridge from "secret bunker reveal" to a tap-timing hoop-scoring loop, so any attempt would feel bait-and-switch and likely tank IPM despite the curiosity hook's broad appeal. Skip this archetype unless we're testing a deliberate misdirection meta-creative, and even then the cluster has only 1 ad / 1 advertiser — too thin a signal to justify production spend.

The "fail with a crashing bus" hook is rooted in puzzle/parking/draw-line gameplay and has zero overlap with Flappy Dunk's tap-to-fly hoop loop — there are no vehicles, no cursor-drag mechanic, and no level-fail moment to misrepresent honestly. Palette (white/green/red) and the cursor-driven visual language also clash with the game's bold magenta/beige 2D cartoon VS aesthetic. Audience (hypercasual fail-bait viewers) does broadly overlap, but adapting this archetype would require a full re-skin to a "tap-and-miss-the-hoop" fail concept, at which point it's no longer this cluster — recommend skipping.

brief.title: "Ever Played Something This Satisfying?" — Hoop-Chain Combo Reskin
brief.hook_3s: "Hey! Ever played a game that's actually this satisfying?" — ball swooshes through three magenta hoops in a row, combo counter EXPLODES on screen.
scene_flow: 0-3s HOOK: Phone-in-hand POV on warm beige court background; finger taps screen, ball flap-bounces through first magenta hoop with a crisp swoosh as VO drops the 'Hey! Ever played...' opener.
3-7s ESCALATE: Camera tightens; ball chains through 3 hoops back-to-back, blue accent combo trails streak across screen, x2 → x3 → x5 multiplier numbers pop in flat-shaded cartoon style.
7-11s VS MOMENT: Screen splits — player score racing ahead of opponent score in classic Flappy Dunk VS HUD; magenta banner flashes 'COMBO!' as the lead widens.
11-14s PAYOFF: Final perfect swoosh, hearts indicator full, confetti burst in primary palette, score lands on a clean round number with a satisfying chime.
14-15s CTA: Logo lockup on beige with magenta 'PLAY FREE' button pulsing.
brief.visual_direction: Lock to Flappy Dunk's signature 2D cartoon DNA: thick black outlines, flat shading, warm beige (#EAD5A0) court backdrop, saturated magenta (#D81155) hoops and banners, bright blue (#5CB3FF) ball trail and combo accents. Keep the authentic in-game HUD — VS bar at top, score counter, hearts indicator — visible at all times so viewers recognize the actual game. No 3D, no realistic shading, no stylus-hand POV; the only finger shown is a real thumb tapping the bottom edge of the phone frame.
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Flappy Dunk rotation leans on generic "best basketball game" video claims and a silent playable — zero satisfaction-pitch or VO-opener hooks. This brief imports the trending "Hey! Ever played a game that..." opener (proven across 2 advertisers) but reskins it onto authentic hoop-chain combo footage, so we keep the archetype's scroll-stopping curiosity bait without the puzzle-game bait-and-switch that would tank D1. UA team: A/B this against the YouTube "Best basketball game ever" cuts — same audience, fresher emotional angle.
scenario_prompts: Vertical 9:16 mobile gameplay frame of Flappy Dunk: a bright blue (#5CB3FF) basketball mid-air arcing through a saturated magenta (#D81155) hoop against a warm beige (#EAD5A0) court background, thick black outlines, flat shading, vibrant high-contrast 2D cartoon style, authentic in-game HUD visible — VS score bar at top showing player ahead, hearts indicator, combo multiplier 'x3' popping in chunky cartoon numerals, blue motion trail behind the ball, bold on-screen text overlay 'Ever played something THIS satisfying?' in white with black outline at upper third, no characters, no stylus, no hands. [Audio: VO 'Hey! Ever played a game that's actually this satisfying?' / punchy upbeat 120bpm arcade trap with playful synth bells / crisp swoosh on hoop pass at 1.0s, rising chime on combo at 2.5s]

Vertical 9:16 Flappy Dunk gameplay still: three magenta (#D81155) hoops stacked diagonally across a warm beige (#EAD5A0) sky, a glowing blue (#5CB3FF) ball streaking through all three with a ribbon-like trail, thick black outlines, flat shading, vibrant high-contrast 2D cartoon style, authentic Flappy Dunk HUD with score counter rapidly ticking up and 'COMBO x5 🔥' banner flashing in magenta, hearts indicator full in corner, on-screen overlay text 'Tap. Flap. Swoosh.' in bold white-with-black-outline cartoon font. [Audio: VO 'Three in a row — wait, FIVE?!' / energetic 128bpm competitive arcade beat with hand-claps / triple swoosh staccato then combo explosion SFX at 3s]

Vertical 9:16 Flappy Dunk VS-screen moment: split-screen score comparison UI with player side glowing blue (#5CB3FF) leading 47-12 over opponent on warm beige (#EAD5A0) background, magenta (#D81155) banner across middle reading 'BEAT YOUR RIVAL', thick black outlines, flat shading, vibrant high-contrast 2D cartoon style, authentic Flappy Dunk hoops and ball visible mid-action behind the HUD, hearts indicator and combo meter present, big rounded magenta 'PLAY FREE' button pulsing at bottom with on-screen overlay text 'PLAY FREE'. [Audio: VO 'Your move.' / triumphant 130bpm competitive sting with crowd-cheer accent / victory chime at 1s, button-tap click at 4s]

brief.title: Flappy Dunk · "Why This Hoop Is So Hard" Fail Bait
brief.hook_3s: A player taps too late — the ball clips the rim, ricochets off three hoops and faceplants on the beige court. "Bet you can't beat this."
scene_flow: 0-3s: Cold open mid-match. VS HUD visible (player vs opponent score 0-0). A finger taps; ball arcs toward a magenta hoop and CLANGS the rim — slow-mo wobble.
3-7s: Ball pinballs between two more hoops, lives/hearts indicator drains from 3 to 1. Big text overlay: 'WHY THIS LEVEL IS SO HARD'.
7-11s: Cursor-style finger retries — taps too early, ball nosedives past the final hoop, opponent scores. Red 'MISS' stamp + combo break shatter FX.
11-14s: Quick cut montage of three near-miss attempts ending in cartoon faceplants on the beige court.
14-15s: Freeze on losing scoreboard. CTA banner slams in: 'CAN YOU WIN?' with Install button.
brief.visual_direction: Lean fully into the Flappy Dunk DNA: warm beige (#EAD5A0) court background, saturated magenta (#D81155) hoops and banners, bright blue (#5CB3FF) ball trail and accent UI. Thick black outlines on every object, flat shading, no gradients. Keep the authentic VS matchmaking HUD, lives/hearts row, and combo counter on-screen at all times. Camera stays locked in the standard side-on gameplay framing — the only "ad" layer is bold sans-serif overlay text and a giant cursor-finger to sell the fail-bait trope.
text_overlays: WHY THIS HOOP IS SO HARD 😤
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Flappy Dunk rotation is all "best basketball game" brag-video and silent playables — they have ZERO fail-bait creatives, despite fail-in-game being a proven hypercasual scroll-stopper. This brief plugs that gap by importing the archetype's challenge-frame ("can you win?") while staying mechanically honest to tap-timing hoop play. UA team: A/B this against the YouTube brag videos on TikTok/Reels where fail-bait outperforms boast-led copy.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, aspect 9:16, authentic Flappy Dunk in-game screenshot: blue basketball mid-bounce clipping the rim of a magenta (#D81155) hoop on a warm beige (#EAD5A0) court background, bright blue (#5CB3FF) motion trail, thick black outlines, flat shading, vibrant high-contrast colors, 2D cartoon style. On-screen HUD: VS scoreboard top-center showing 0-2, three hearts in top-left with one greyed out, combo counter '0x'. Giant white cursor-finger overlay tapping the screen. Bold black sans-serif overlay text: 'WHY THIS HOOP IS SO HARD 😤'. No human character. [Audio: VO (frustrated gen-z) 'bro this level is RIGGED' / punchy upbeat trap 128bpm with competitive snare rolls / clang-on-rim at 0.8s, whoosh on cursor tap, fail-buzzer at 4.5s]

Storyboard frame, aspect 9:16, Flappy Dunk gameplay still: blue ball nosediving below a magenta hoop ring, beige (#EAD5A0) court, blue (#5CB3FF) trail breaking apart into particles, red 'MISS!' stamp slammed across center, magenta (#D81155) banner at bottom, thick black outlines, flat shading, 2D cartoon style, vibrant high-contrast colors. HUD shows lives indicator at 1 heart, opponent score ticking up. Overlay text: 'TAP TOO LATE…'. [Audio: VO 'NO WAY' / energetic competitive trap 128bpm / shatter SFX on miss, crowd 'ooooh' sting at 2s, heart-loss blip at 3s]

Storyboard frame, aspect 9:16, Flappy Dunk end-card: losing scoreboard center-screen on warm beige (#EAD5A0) background, magenta (#D81155) CTA banner reading 'CAN YOU WIN?' with bright blue (#5CB3FF) Install button, blue basketball deflated next to a magenta hoop, thick black outlines, flat shading, vibrant high-contrast 2D cartoon style. Bold overlay text: 'CAN YOU WIN?'. No character on screen. [Audio: VO (taunting) 'only 3% finish this — prove me wrong' / triumphant competitive trap drop 130bpm / button-tap chime at 1s, coin-jingle on CTA reveal at 2.5s]

brief.title: Hero Pose: Champion Ball vs. Rival
brief.hook_3s: A glowing basketball charges up mid-air between two hoops as a magenta "VS" banner slams onto the screen — PLAY NOW!
scene_flow: 0-3s: Hero frame — the Flappy Dunk basketball hovers center-screen between two hoops, crackling with blue energy aura, beige court backdrop, giant magenta 'PLAY NOW!' banner slams in.
3-7s: Quick gameplay cut — finger taps, ball flap-bounces through three hoops in rapid succession, score counter ticks 1→2→3 with combo flash.
7-11s: Split-screen VS — player ball (blue trail) vs rival ball (magenta trail) racing through parallel hoop lanes, score comparison UI on top.
11-14s: Player ball nails a buzzer-beater hoop, rival ball clips the rim and falls, 'WINNER!' stamp explodes in magenta.
14-15s: End card — logo + giant blue 'PLAY NOW!' CTA button pulsing.
brief.visual_direction: Lean fully into Flappy Dunk's signature 2D cartoon look: thick black outlines, flat shading, warm beige (#EAD5A0) court/sky background, saturated magenta (#D81155) banners and rival accents, bright blue (#5CB3FF) energy aura and player trail. Hero frame mimics a comic-book splash page — static, iconic, poster-like — so the basketball reads as the "character" the way Goku does in the source archetype. Keep the in-game HUD (score counter, lives/hearts, combo burst) visible to anchor authenticity.
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Flappy Dunk rotation is all gameplay-video and a single playable — they have zero static "hero pose + giant CTA" poster creatives, which is exactly the underrepresented archetype trending in the scan. We adapt the Goku-splash formula by treating the basketball itself as the iconic hero (no IP risk, no invented character) and lean on the game's native magenta banner DNA. UA team: run this as a thumbnail-first asset on networks where strong static stopping power outperforms gameplay loops (Meta, TikTok feed).
scenario_prompts: Hero splash frame, aspect 9:16, 2D cartoon style with thick black outlines, flat shading, and vibrant, high-contrast colors. A glowing orange basketball hovers center-frame between two cartoon basketball hoops with magenta nets, crackling blue (#5CB3FF) energy aura swirling around it, warm beige (#EAD5A0) sky background with simple flat clouds, in-game Flappy Dunk score counter '00' top-center and tiny heart lives indicator top-left, giant bold magenta (#D81155) banner across lower third reading 'PLAY NOW!' in chunky white outlined letters, poster-style composition, no human character. [Audio: VO 'Can you beat the champ? Play now!' / punchy upbeat trap 128 BPM with brassy hit / energy-charge whoosh at 0.5s, deep bass drop on banner slam at 1.2s]

Storyboard frame — combo moment, aspect 9:16, 2D cartoon style with thick black outlines, flat shading, and vibrant, high-contrast colors. Side-view of the Flappy Dunk basketball mid-flap arcing through a magenta (#D81155) hoop with net swooshing, bright blue (#5CB3FF) motion trail behind it, two more hoops lined up to the right, beige (#EAD5A0) background, score UI '03' top-center with combo burst star, bold white-outlined overlay text '3X COMBO!' diagonal across upper-right in magenta. [Audio: no VO / energetic competitive synth-trap 130 BPM / triple chime ascending on each hoop pass, crowd cheer swell at 1s]

Storyboard frame — VS split moment, aspect 9:16, 2D cartoon style with thick black outlines, flat shading, and vibrant, high-contrast colors. Vertical split-screen: left lane shows blue-trailed player basketball clearing a hoop, right lane shows magenta-trailed rival basketball clipping the rim, jagged black 'VS' bolt down the center seam, beige (#EAD5A0) court backdrop both sides, score comparison UI top reading 'YOU 4 — RIVAL 2', bold magenta (#D81155) banner bottom reading 'WINNER!'. [Audio: VO 'You win!' triumphant / competitive trap 132 BPM with stadium horn / rim-clang fail SFX right side at 0.8s, victory chime left side at 1.5s]
